title: App Readiness Passport
description: A small, descriptive, tool-naming fingerprint for cross-comparing many apps at a glance: what an app is, what it is built on, how ready it is for LLM-automated development, and how ready it is for production. Sibling to the vendor-neutral .ai/manifest.yaml (which is the agent-facing CONTRACT); the passport is the human/portfolio-facing SCORECARD. Readers MUST ignore fields they do not recognize.

passport.description: Stable schema id (not a URL, so it can't rot).

passportVersion.description: Semver of THIS schema. Additive within a major; readers ignore unknown fields.

generatedBy.description: Who/what produced it: 'manual', a scanner name, a CI job, etc.

slug.description: Stable, sortable key for portfolio tables.

purpose.description: One line on what this app is for.

archetype.description: How the repo is run — selects the readiness lens.

license.description: SPDX id or 'none' (legal blocker) or null if unknown.

criticality.description: How much a failure costs — frames how to read the readiness scores.

stack.description: The tool-NAMING block. Unlike .ai/manifest.yaml this DELIBERATELY names concrete tools — that is the whole point of a first-sight comparison.

persistence.description: Each datastore the app relies on. Empty array = stateless.

required.description: false if the app degrades gracefully without it.

monitoring.description: Named observability vendors/tools. nulls are meaningful — they say 'this is absent'.

integrations.description: Every external service the app talks to. The 'kind' is the comparable axis; 'name' is the vendor.

auth.description: How the app authenticates to it: 'oauth', 'api-key', 'iam', 'webhook-secret', ...

secretsFrom.description: Where secrets legitimately come from (vault/keyring/env), never the secrets themselves.

automationReadiness.description: Readiness for FULL LLM-automated development: can an agent understand the repo, act in it, and verify its own change before a human looks? Reuses Ascent's L1..L5 ladder.

level.description: L1 Manual, L2 Assisted, L3 Augmented, L4 Integrated, L5 Autonomous.

artifacts.description: The agent-facing inputs an autonomous agent needs to be effective.

contextGraph.description: Machine-readable map of modules->features (context-map.json, CONTEXT.md coverage).

manifest.description: Carries an agent-facing capability manifest (.ai/manifest.yaml or equivalent).

evals.description: Golden/eval tests for any LLM-produced output.

skills.description: Reusable agent skills / subagents / prompt library.

selfVerify.description: Which capabilities an agent can run LOCALLY to prove a change before push. The more true, the safer autonomy.

aiInWorkflow.description: Evidence AI is actually used (AI co-author trailers, agent-authored PRs), not just configured.

blockers.description: What caps the level — the highest-leverage things to fix next.

productionReadiness.description: Readiness to be TRUSTED in production. Each component is an ordinal enum so a portfolio sorts trivially; 'score' and 'band' are the headline.

level.description: none -> build-only -> runs checks (non-blocking) -> checks GATE merge -> automated deploy -> progressive/canary+reversible.
none -> a few -> meaningful but gaps -> critical paths covered -> broad unit+integration+e2e with coverage tracked.

coveragePct.description: Measured line/branch coverage, or null if not tracked.

level.description: none -> SECURITY.md only -> secret/dep scanning present -> SAST/SCA GATES merges -> + SBOM/signing/provenance.
none -> structured logs -> error tracking -> + metrics/dashboards -> + distributed tracing/alerting.

rollback.description: A defined, tested way back (versioned deploys, feature flags).

links.description: Pointers to richer artifacts so the passport stays thin.

report.description: Latest full maturity/readiness report this was summarized from.

evidence.description: Provenance, so a reader knows how much to trust the scores.

confidence.description: 0..1 — how much of the app could be inspected.

files.description: Files the assessment was synthesized from — drift-check these.
